description_full:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Digital Analytics Specialist (MarTech) in Germany.

This role focuses on building and maintaining reliable, end-to-end tracking systems that ensure accurate measurement across marketing, CRM, and web platforms. You will work at the intersection of marketing, analytics, and engineering, translating business requirements into robust technical tracking implementations. The position requires strong attention to detail and the ability to ensure data integrity across complex, multi-platform ecosystems. You will be responsible for implementing and validating event tracking, troubleshooting attribution issues, and ensuring consistency in reporting across tools. Working closely with account managers, engineers, and clients, you will play a key role in shaping trustworthy data foundations for marketing performance. This is a highly technical and impactful role for someone who enjoys solving complex tracking challenges and improving data reliability at scale.

Accountabilities


Implement and maintain tracking systems including Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, Google Ads conversion tracking, and related MarTech tools.
Design, configure, and QA event tracking across websites, CMS platforms, CRMs, and eCommerce environments.
Validate conversion events, UTMs, and attribution logic to ensure accurate cross-platform measurement.
Integrate tracking systems with CRMs such as HubSpot, Salesforce, GoHighLevel, or Recruiterflow.
Work directly with JavaScript, TypeScript, and React-based codebases when tag managers are insufficient.
Troubleshoot and resolve cross-platform tracking, attribution, and data flow issues.
Conduct rigorous QA testing of tracking implementations and ensure accuracy before deployment.
Document tracking setups, assumptions, and limitations clearly for internal teams and clients.
Collaborate with account managers and stakeholders to gather requirements and recommend improvements to tracking systems.


Requirements


3-5+ years of experience in digital analytics, tracking implementation, or technical ad operations roles.
Strong hands-on expertise with Google Tag Manager, Meta Pixel, and Google Ads tracking setups.
Proficiency in JavaScript and TypeScript, with experience working in React-based environments.
Solid ability to diagnose and resolve complex tracking, attribution, and data accuracy issues.
Experience validating and maintaining multi-platform event tracking and marketing measurement systems.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain technical concepts to non-technical stakeholders.
Detail-oriented mindset with strong QA discipline and documentation skills.
Ability to manage multiple tracking environments in fast-paced, client-facing settings.
Nice to have: experience with server-side GTM, advanced tracking architectures, or broader MarTech ecosystems.


Benefits


Fully remote role, offering flexibility to work from anywhere.
Competitive compensation paid in USD on a regular schedule.
Paid time off in accordance with company policy.
Observance of official holidays per company guidelines.
Opportunity to work directly with engineers, growth marketers, and account teams.
High-impact role with ownership over marketing data accuracy and tracking infrastructure.
Exposure to modern MarTech stacks and complex analytics environments.
Collaborative, fast-paced, and technically driven work environment.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. Wir gehören zu den Top-Adressen in der IT-Welt und fokussieren uns auf die Optimierung der Kerngeschäftsprozesse unserer Kunden. DEINE ROLLE


Analytics-Konzeption: Entwickle Analytics-Strategien und konzipiere individuelle Lösungen für komplexe Herausforderungen in Web- und App-Projekten.
Implementierung: Implementiere Analytics-Tools und richte Tag-Management-, Tracking- und Consent-Management-Systeme für unsere Kunden ein.
Reporting & Dashboards: Erstelle Berichte und interaktive Dashboards, visualisiere Ergebnisse und interpretiere diese gemeinsam mit internen Teams im Online Marketing.
Compliance & Prozesse: Optimiere Prozesse und unterstütze unsere Kunden dabei, technische und rechtliche Anforderungen wie die Cookie-Compliance einzuhalten.
Wissensvermittlung: Vermittle Wissen, indem du interne und externe Schulungen zu Analytics-Themen durchführst.
Strategische Beratung: Berate Kunden und begleite sie beim strategischen Ausbau ihres Trackings.


DEIN PROFIL


Analytics- und Tracking-Systeme: Durch deine mehrjährige Berufserfahrung (mind. 3 Jahre) kennst du bereits Tools wie Piwik PRO, Matomo oder Google Analytics und bist mit Tag-Management- und Dashboard-Systemen vertraut. Aber auch Consent Management Plattformen sind dir nicht unbekannt und du verstehst die Funktion von Cookie Bannern auf Webseiten und in Mobile Apps.
Online-Marketing Expertise: Insbesondere datengetriebene Kampagnen gehören zu deinem Fachwissen.
Technisches Verständnis: Du bringst fundiertes Know-how in Webtechnologien und Programmiersprachen wie HTML, CSS und JavaScript mit.
Kenntnisse zur strategischen Analytics-Beratung: Idealerweise bringst du erste Erfahrung mit ganzheitlichen Datenstrategien für Unternehmen mit und bist begeisterungsfähig für technologische Entwicklungen und Trends im Analytics-Bereich.
Kommunikationsstärke: Du bist vertriebsorientiert und begeisterst andere für das Thema Digital Marketing.
KI Kompetenz: Du setzt KI-Tools in deinem Fachgebiet souverän ein, bewertest die Ergebnisse kritisch und unterstützt Kunden und adessi dabei, Potenziale von KI zu nutzen.

Unser Versprechen: Du wirst dich bei uns wohlfühlen! Kollegial, familiär und auf Augenhöhe – wir leben Austausch, Teamgeist und einen respektvollen Umgang miteinander. Diversität und verschiedene Blickwinkel werden bei uns genauso geschätzt wie du als Mensch. Das und viel mehr steht für unser ganz besonderes Wir-Gefühl. Für das es sogar ein Wort gibt: adessi.

Unsere Kultur und das Zusammenarbeiten sind geprägt von gegenseitiger Wertschätzung, Anerkennung und Unterstützung. Das verbindet uns hierarchieübergreifend – auch im Home-Office. So belegen wir zum wiederholten Mal den 1. Platz beim Wettbewerb „Deutschlands Beste Arbeitgeber in der ITK“!. Unser umfangreiches Trainingsangebot und ein transparentes Laufbahnstufenmodell für alle sorgen dafür, dass deine Entwicklung bei uns nicht stillsteht. Denn gemeinsam zu wachsen und Chancengeber zu sein, das ist programmiert in unserer DNA.

description_full:
Job Title: Sales Operations Reporting & Analytics Specialist

Locations: Berlin, Dordrecht, London, Singapore and New Delhi

Application Deadline:  6th May 2026

About Springer Nature

Springer Nature opens the doors to discovery for researchers, educators, clinicians and other professionals. Every day, around the globe, our imprints, books, journals, platforms and technology solutions reach millions of people. For over 180 years our brands and imprints have been a trusted source of knowledge to these communities and today, more than ever, we see it as our responsibility to ensure that fundamental knowledge can be found, verified, understood and used by our communities – enabling them to improve outcomes, make progress, and benefit the generations that follow. Visit group.springernature.com and follow @SpringerNature

About The Role

The Springer Nature Sales Operations Centre of Excellence (SOCoE) is a Dordrecht, London, Berlin, and Heidelberg based team, operating in the academic publishing industry. We are part of our worldwide sales organisation and are tasked to improve the way we work in our day-to-day operations of delivering online scientific content to our customers. The Sales Operations Reporting & Analytics Specialist creates reports and dashboards, analyse metrics, and reports data to the Senior VPs and high-level management. This role is responsible for providing a comprehensive assessment and overall evaluation of reporting deliverables using various systems and visualisation tools.

In the day-to-day business this person will typically work closely with the internal Sales, Sales Operations, and IT departments. Jointly they work on continuous improvement of workflows and the way our services are delivered to the customers.

A significant part of the work will consist of innovating the data and reporting processes and user interface of the CRM systems. The requirements of these improvements will be defined together with stakeholders and key-users.

Purpose of the role:

Creating reports and dashboards, providing data analytics, and implementing projects to drive operational change based on best practices as part of the continuous improvement strategy, whilst supporting business as usual development within the institutional sales organisation.

Role Responsibilities:


Create and maintain new report templates and dashboards either upon user request or own initiative
Research and analyse operational data to identify areas of improvement
Assist in expanding the financial reporting platform across the organisation (Salesforce, Data Studio, GBQ, SAP Query Designer, CRM Analytics)
Train relevant team members on the different reporting platforms.
Project manage against set timelines and deliverables
Contribute to strategic direction for operational improvements
Support Sales and Sales Operations with continuous improvement
Proactively initiate projects that align and support the strategic objectives
Implement and manage change processes
Manage and translate requirements of change from business to IT
Provide excellent communication acting as a liaison for key stakeholders
Provide (online) training to the stakeholder community during the implementation of change
Support the monthly process of loading Institutional sales into Intelis (manage process when required)
Contribute to the development of Intelis to meet the needs of the stakeholders
Contribute to the logic for the calculation of allocated revenue in Salesforce
Analyse IT tools and suggest changes needed to be able to support initiatives.


Key relationships:


The Sales Operations Reporting & Analytics Specialist will report to the Director of the Sales Operations Centre of Excellence and be part of the Sales Operations Centre of Excellence team.


Experience, Skills & Qualifications

Essential:


Minimum 6 years’ experience in data analysis, with strong analytical skills and commercial acumen
Experience with CRM systems and data analysis tools (e.g. GBQ, Looker, Google Analytics, Excel)
Solid financial understanding and ability to interpret business data
Project, process, and workflow analysis skills from planning through implementation
Proactive self-starter and problem solver with strong attention to detail
Excellent communication and interpersonal skills; fluent in English (written and verbal)
Team player with a flexible, reliable, results-oriented working style and international/cultural sensitivity


Desirable:


Previous experience in Academic Publishing industry
Relevant Masters degree in data analytics or similar
Consulting skills and experience
Project management qualifications (e.g. Prince2, agile scrum, etc.)
Python knowledge would be an asset


Springer Nature Skills associated with this job profile include SN-Accounting Principles, SN-Communicates Effectively, SN-Data analytics / Quantitative Skills, SN-Financial/Tax/Capital Market Regulations, SN-Financial Acumen, SN-Resourcefulness, SN-Tech Savvy

At Springer Nature, our mission is to be part of progress – and that begins with inclusion: of people, perspectives, and ideas. We believe that diverse perspectives drive progress, and we are committed to creating an environment where people and ideas can flourish. If you have any access needs related to disability, neurodivergence or a chronic condition, please contact us so we can make all necessary accommodation. Find out more about our DEI work here: https://group.springernature.com/gp/group/taking-responsibility/diversity-equity-inclusion.

For more information about career opportunities in Springer Nature please visit https://springernature.wd3.myworkdayjobs.com/SpringerNatureCareers/.

description_full:
Do you thrive in fast-paced environments and have a passion for renewable energy?

As our Go-to-Market AI & Data Associate, you will be the co-architect of our data ecosystem. You’ll take full ownership of making customer and revenue data accessible, building internally used applications from the ground up, and deploying AI to supercharge our growth.




Please note that for this role, C2 English proficiency is required. This is a full-time, on-site position based in Berlin.




What You’ll Do

Process Innovation & AI Implementation: You will dive deep into our customer-oriented workflows to reshape them with your creative, AI-driven ideas and technical skills. As the owner of the implementation, you won't just strategize—you will personally drive these improvements from concept to reality.
Vertical Owner (Data Driven Analytics): You will relentlessly improve our data product experience by working with internal and external stakeholders to identify bottlenecks, refine analytical fit, and personally onboard them into a world-class, data-driven workflow.
Data Accessibility: Design and maintain robust pipelines to make data from our customers, HubSpot, and Aircall fully usable and accessible across the organization.
AI Integration: Implement AI-driven workflows and predictive models to automate insights, lead scoring, and trend forecasting.
Hands-on Engineering: Work directly in Python (or TypeScript) to integrate diverse data sources yourself.
Strategic Impact: Collaborate with Sales, Marketing, and Success teams to transform complex datasets into powerful BI dashboards that drive faster, smarter decisions.




What Makes You a Great Fit

You are hungry, driven, and eager to find smart, efficient solutions. You don't just accept the status quo—you constantly look for ways to innovate and optimize.
You are an expert in using n8n, Python (TypeScript is a bonus) and have a deep understanding of modern BI tools and SQL.
You are passionate about how AI can optimize revenue processes and have the skills to implement it.
Bonus: Ideally, you have experience in BI or Data Engineering, with a proven track record of building data warehouses and integrating SaaS tools (like HubSpot or Aircall)
You are proactive and structured, you don’t wait for a ticket; you identify bottlenecks in our data flow and fix them.
You are fluent in English. Proficiency in German, Italian, French or Portuguese is a plus.




Why Join Us?

Be part of one of the fastest growing VC-backed startup in Germany with 2000+ satisfied B2B customers.
A vibrant, collaborative office culture where great ideas happen face-to-face — we're a full in-person team (5 days/week) in a beautiful Berlin office near Hackescher Markt.
Enjoy opportunities for personal and professional growth.
Receive competitive pay.
Perks include gym membership, coffee, regular team events, MacBook and more.
Embrace the freedom to innovate and make quick changes in a dynamic team.




Even if you don’t meet every requirement, we encourage you to apply! Imposter syndrome shouldn't hold you back—we value talent and passion above all.




About Reonic

At Reonic, we’re on a mission to simplify renewable technology with our B2B SaaS solutions for solar, storage, and heat pumps. Backed by top-tier investors and trusted by 2000+ business customers, we’re growing fast—and we need YOU to help us accommodate our new and existing customers.

description_full:
Data Scientist – Analytics, Statistics & BI - €120,000




A growing AI-first HealthTech company in the Frankfurt Rhine-Main region is hiring a Data Scientist - Analytics, Statistics & BI.




This is not a pure ML engineering role. It’s a senior analytics position focused on rigorous statistical thinking, production data systems, and real-world healthcare impact.




You’ll take ownership of the analytics layer behind a live AI platform already used in therapeutic environments. Working with complex behavioural and clinical datasets, you’ll apply strong statistical methodologies to identify meaningful patterns, evaluate study outcomes, and translate data into insights that directly influence product and clinical decision-making.




This is a senior individual contributor role with real influence - partnering closely with AI, engineering and product teams while driving BI, data quality standards, and the evolution of the data warehouse environment.




What you’ll bring:

5+ years’ experience as a Data Scientist with strong analytics and statistical focus
Excellent foundation in applied statistics (hypothesis testing, regression modelling, study evaluation)
Strong Python and solid SQL
Experience working with modern data warehouses (BigQuery ideal)
Experience owning or evolving BI environments (Superset, Looker, Power BI etc.)
AI-ready mindset – actively leveraging modern AI tools to improve workflows




Why join?

Meaningful AI impact in pediatric HealthTech
Live production system with real-world usage
High ownership and visibility in a 30-person company
Hybrid setup in the Frankfurt Rhine-Main region
Bonus, stock options, 30 days holiday + strong benefits package




If you’re a statistically rigorous, product-minded Data Scientist who wants ownership and genuine impact - let’s talk.




Keywords: Senior Data Scientist, Analytics, Statistical Modelling, Python, SQL, BigQuery, GCP, Business Intelligence, HealthTech, AI Systems

#SAPNextGen

Gib dein Bestes

SAP-Innovationen helfen mehr als vierhunderttausend Kunden weltweit, effizienter zusammenzuarbeiten und Business Insight effektiver zu nutzen. SAP war ursprünglich für seine Führungsrolle in der ERP-Software (Enterprise Resource Planning) bekannt und hat sich zu einem Marktführer für End-to-End-Software für Geschäftsanwendungen und zugehörige Services für Datenbank, Analyse, intelligente Technologien und Erfahrungsmanagement entwickelt. Bei SAP kannst du dein Bestes geben.

In unserer Kultur der Inklusion stehen Gesundheit und Wohlbefinden im Mittelpunkt. Flexible Arbeitsmodelle sorgen dafür, dass sich jede und jeder – unabhängig vom persönlichen Hintergrund – eingebunden fühlt und Bestleistungen erbringen kann. Wir bei SAP sind davon überzeugt, dass wir durch die einzigartigen Fähigkeiten und Qualitäten, die jede und jeder in unser Unternehmen einbringt, stärker werden. Wir investieren in unsere Mitarbeitenden, um ihr Selbstvertrauen zu stärken und ihnen dabei zu helfen, ihr volles Potenzial zu entfalten. Wir setzen auf die Förderung aller Talente, um eine bessere und gerechtere Welt zu schaffen.

Bei uns können Sie Ihre Fähigkeiten einsetzen und im Rahmen von Personalentwicklungsprogrammen Ihre Potentiale weiterentwickeln und ausschöpfen. Ihr Gehalt bei HEIDELBERG wird attraktiv sein, Ihr Arbeitsumfeld modern und Ihre sozialen Leistungen sehr gut.

description_full:
Founded in 2017, refurbed is Europe’s fastest-growing marketplace for refurbished products, active in 12 European countries and having surpassed €2Bn in GMV — all while being profitable. With beautiful headquarters in Vienna, we operate as a remote-first company, and our 250+ employees enjoy up to two months of workation per year. We’ve also been recognised as a Top DACH Employer by Kununu for three consecutive years.




Our mission is to make sustainable consumption the easy choice by enabling customers to buy products up to 40% cheaper while significantly reducing CO₂ emissions.




If you thrive in an environment that values momentum, ownership, and impact, you’ll feel at home here. We’re a fast-paced, high-performance team that works hard and challenges itself everyday. To enable this high-performance every team member enjoys full autonomy over their location (we’re remote-first).




The Category and Sales Marketing Analyst will play a crucial role in supporting vouchers and promotion operations, multi-category growth, improving supply-demand alignment, and strengthening the commercial foundations needed for successful marketing strategies across multiple categories, markets, and performance channels.




This role will directly enable Refurbed’s expansion across categories, brands and countries by taking ownership of operational and analytical tasks.




You will work closely with Demand Planning, Category Managers, CRM, Performance Marketing (Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates), Product, and Commercial teams.




WHO YOU ARE
2–5 years experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial strategy.
Strong analytical and data-handling skills; able to create clear insights from complex data.
Highly organized, reliable, and operationally strong.
Comfortable coordinating with multiple teams and managing parallel workstreams across categories, markets, and performance channels.
Proactive mindset with strong problem-solving skills.
Experience with consumer electronics / SDA is a plus.
Experience with promotions or marketplace operations preferred.
Bonus: performance marketing fundamentals (e.g., Search/Shopping, Paid Social, Affiliates)




Location & eligibility: This role is open to candidates based in Europe (including UK) only with a valid right to work.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

You drive multi-category growth. You support prioritization and budget allocation across categories and markets, turning demand-side insights (search, click data, pricing signals) into clear, actionable growth opportunities and structured recommendations.
You run promotions end-to-end. You execute voucher campaigns across markets, monitor performance and ROI, and continuously optimize based on supply relevance, cost efficiency, and commercial impact.
You connect Marketing and Supply. You align teams by consolidating forecasts, stock, pricing, and partner inputs, ensuring everyone works off the same priorities and timelines while contributing clear insights to business reviews.
You enable brands and partners. You maintain key KPIs, ensure data readiness for activation, and support smooth rollout of brand campaigns.
You scale how we work. You build tools, dashboards, and documentation that enable teams to be more self-serve, while identifying automation opportunities to reduce manual effort.
You support performance execution. You help translate category priorities into performance marketing channels (Search, Paid Social, Affiliates) and ensure campaigns are set up for success.




WHY YOU WILL ENJOY WORKING WITH US:

Our Culture and Values:

Decisions should be made based on facts and not by hierarchy levels? We sure think so!
We embrace giving and receiving feedback, testing and welcoming new ideas, as well as sharing our knowledge across the company.
You fail sometimes? Well, so do we! We’re all just human, let’s learn from our mistakes to improve in the future!
No matter who you love, where you’re from, who you pray to, whether you pee sitting down or standing up – we are a bunch of talented people who enjoy spending our time making a difference!

Personal & Professional Development:

You'll have access to a dedicated learning budget within your department to support your growth, whether it's courses, conferences, or other learning opportunities that matter to you.
We partner with Likeminded, offering you private sessions with experts for your personal growth and mental health.
We frequently host internal workshops, allowing our teams to further develop their skills in different areas and enhancing collaboration across departments.

Flexibility:

We care about a healthy work/life balance. We not only function and support a fully remote setting but also offer the possibility to work from our beautiful office in the vibrant heart of Vienna – we love dogs, so feel free to bring your furry little buddy too 😊
We trust you to do the best job, so we don't care so much when you do it – you are able to self-organize.
You will also be invited to take part in our office weeks, happening three times a year, where you will have the chance to work side by side with your colleagues and enjoy the team-building events and on-site workshops




…and much more! See https://careers.refurbed.com/ for further insights!

Als junges IT-Start-up – organisch gewachsen aus einem (vormals ausschließlich) internen Servicecenter – liefern wir heute maßgeschneiderte Softwarelösungen und IT-Services für die Freizeit- und Tourismusbranche. Unser Fokus liegt auf der Entwicklung innovativer Systeme und immersiver Erlebnisse, die uns und den Markt voranbringen.

Werde Teil unseres Teams und trage zu unserem gemeinsamen Erfolg bei. Was uns ausmacht:

Wir lieben unseren Job: Wir brennen für dieselbe Sache, unser gemeinsames Thema „Wellbeing“ vereint uns und treibt uns an.
Wir pflegen eine offene Kultur: Flache Hierarchien und ein direkter Austausch auf Augenhöhe prägen unsere Arbeit und machen uns agil.
Wir sind familiär und familienfreundlich: Vertrautheit und Teamgeist beschreiben unser Miteinander. Dabei sind und bleiben wir menschlich.
Wir lernen aus Erfahrungen: Eine gute Fehlerkultur bringt uns nach vorne. Lösungen suchen wir als Team.
Wir übernehmen Verantwortung: Mit unserer Arbeit finanzieren wir gemeinnützige Projekte unserer Stiftung und Investitionen in nachhaltige Entwicklungen.
Bei uns kannst du Dinge bewegen. Wir laden dich zum aktiven Mitgestalten ein.





Der Job auf einen Blick



Wir bieten dir von Anfang an ein unbefristetes Arbeitsverhältnis mit einer Arbeitszeit von 40h pro Woche und folgenden Aufgaben:

Planung und Umsetzung von Direktmarketingmaßnahmen (Newsletter, App-Push, Automatisierungen)
Analyse & Monitoring relevanter KPIs und Ableitung von Optimierungsmaßnahmen
Erstellung von Reportings inkl. Handlungsempfehlungen
Unterstützung bei Performance-Marketing-Kampagnen
Eigenständige Konzeption und Steuerung von Social Media Ads
Aktive Weiterentwicklung unseres MyBluphoria Treueprogramms
Bearbeitung von Gastanfragen
Unterstützung beim Social Media Community-Management
Pflege von Artikeln im Webshop und in der App
Zusammenarbeit mit internen Teams und externen Partnern






Skills und Werte die zu uns passen


Studium in Marketing, BWL, E-Commerce oder vergleichbar
Erste Berufserfahrung im Online-Marketing oder E-Commerce
Praktische Erfahrung in der Steuerung und Optimierung von Performance-Marketing-Kampagnen, insbesondere auf Meta (Facebook/Instagram) und TikTok
Analytisches Denken und sicherer Umgang mit Zahlen
Affinität zu datengetriebenem Marketing
Sehr gute Deutsch- und Englischkenntnisse
Sicherer Umgang mit CRM-Systemen (idealerweise HubSpot) & Google Analytics
Selbstständige, zuverlässige und teamorientierte Arbeitsweise
Reisebereitschaft






Das Plus zum Traumjob


Arbeiten in paradiesischer Atmosphäre: Unsere Anlagen bieten dir ein einzigartiges Arbeitsumfeld mit Südsee-Flair unter Palmen.


Exklusive Mitarbeiter-Vorteile: Nutze unsere Produkte und Dienstleistungen zu besonderen Konditionen – ein echtes Plus für dein Wohlbefinden.


Flexibel & fair: Ob Schichtdienst oder Gleitzeit – bei uns findest du faire und moderne Arbeitszeitmodelle, die zu deinem Leben passen.


Ankommen leicht gemacht: Ein strukturiertes Onboarding mit Einarbeitungsplan und Paten-Programm sorgt dafür, dass du dich von Anfang an wohlfühlst.


Individuelle Entwicklungschancen: Wir investieren in dich und fördern dein Wachstum durch persönliche Weiterbildungen mit festem Budget.


Stabilität & Sicherheit: Als Teil einer gewachsenen Unternehmensgruppe profitierst du von einem starken Rückhalt, modernen Strukturen und langfristiger Arbeitsplatzsicherheit.


Mehr fürs Leben: Kita-Zuschuss, Bikeleasing, Firmenfitness und eine betriebliche Altersvorsorge, die über das gesetzliche Maß hinausgeht, geben dir und uns ein gutes Gefühl.


Kostenfreie Getränke & Genuss-Vorteile: Erfrischung geht auf uns – plus attraktive Essensrabatte bei unseren Partnergastronomen.





Kontakt



Diese Ausschreibung richtet sich ausdrücklich auch an Bewerberinnen und Bewerber mit Behinderungen, die wir in unserem Haus stets sehr gerne beschäftigen und willkommen heißen.

Wir machen dir das Bewerben leicht: Lade ganz einfach hier dein Motivationsschreiben in deutscher Sprache, deinen Lebenslauf und deine Zeugnisse hoch, indem du auf "Jetzt bewerben" klickst. Wir freuen uns darauf von dir zu lesen!

Für Rückfragen stehen wir dir gerne unter traumjob@tecreation.de zur Verfügung!



Über uns











Wir sind Deutschlands führender Entwickler und Betreiber von Thermen und Badewelten. Unsere rd. 500 MitarbeiterInnen betreuen unsere Gäste persönlich an unseren drei Wellnessstandorten oder steuern in unseren übergreifenden Kompetenzcentern unsere gruppenweiten Prozesse. Was uns alle verbindet: Teamgeist, Offenheit und der Mut, Neues zu wagen. Vielfalt und soziale Verantwortung sind dabei Teil unserer DNA.
Wir lieben und leben, was wir tun. Und diese Begeisterung spüren unsere Gäste.
Unsere Holding
Unsere Holding mit Sitz in Markdorf ist das organisatorische Herz unserer Unternehmensgruppe. In zentralen Fachbereichen wie Finance, HR, Marketing, Technik und Standortentwicklung bündeln wir Know-how, steuern gruppenweite Prozesse und unterstützen unsere Thermenstandorte als starke Serviceeinheiten – für eine gemeinsame, zukunftsorientierte Entwicklung.
Unsere Thermen & Badewelten
In unseren drei Thermen im Schwarzwald, in Sinsheim und Euskirchen erleben unsere Gäste einzigartige Momente der Entspannung in paradiesischer Atmosphäre. Im Mittelpunkt steht der Mensch: Dafür sorgen unsere engagierten Teams aus Empfang, Gästebetreuung, Guest Relation, Massage Oase und Haustechnik mit Herz und Hingabe an 365 Tagen im Jahr.
Unsere Wellbeing-Marke BLUPHORIA
BLUPHORIA steht für ganzheitliches Wellbeing im Flow – über alle Sinne, Orte und Kanäle hinweg. Als Wellness- und Lifestylemarke entwickeln wir natürliche Pflegeprodukte, hochwertige Badetextilien und immersive Erlebnisse, die Körper, Geist und Seele in Einklang bringen. Mit einem starken Fokus auf Innovation, Storytelling und den echten Mehrwert für unsere Gäste schaffen wir multisensorische Angebote – in den Thermen und Badewelten genauso wie zu Hause. Wir arbeiten als Team Hand in Hand, um BLUPHORIA sowohl digital als auch vor Ort erlebbar zu machen.
Unsere IT-Gesellschaft TeCreation
Mit TeCreation bringen wir Wellbeing in die digitale Zukunft. Als junges IT-Start-up – organisch gewachsen aus einem (vormals ausschließlich) internen Servicecenter – liefern wir heute maßgeschneiderte Softwarelösungen und IT-Services für die Freizeit- und Tourismusbranche. Unser Fokus liegt auf der Entwicklung innovativer Systeme und immersiver Erlebnisse, die unsere Thermengruppe und den Markt voranbringen.
Werde Teil unserer Wellness-Paradiese und gestalte mit uns unvergessliche Gästeerlebnisse!

description_full:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Category & Marketing Analyst - B2C in Germany.

This role sits at the intersection of category growth, marketing execution, and commercial analytics, supporting the scaling of a fast-moving multi-category marketplace across multiple European markets. You will transform demand-side insights, pricing signals, and performance data into actionable recommendations that directly influence growth decisions. Acting as a key connector between marketing, supply, and category teams, you will ensure alignment across priorities, campaigns, and commercial execution. The role is highly operational and analytical, requiring someone who can move between strategy and execution with ease. You will also own the end-to-end execution of promotional mechanics such as voucher campaigns while continuously optimizing performance and ROI. This is a high-impact position where your work will directly shape category expansion, campaign efficiency, and marketplace performance. It is ideal for someone who thrives in fast-paced environments and enjoys turning complex data into structured execution.

Accountabilities


Support multi-category growth by turning demand signals, pricing data, and user behavior insights into actionable growth recommendations.
Own and execute promotional campaigns end-to-end, including voucher setup, monitoring, optimization, and ROI tracking.
Align marketing and supply-side teams by consolidating forecasts, stock availability, pricing inputs, and performance priorities.
Ensure smooth execution of brand and partner campaigns by maintaining KPI tracking and data readiness.
Translate category strategies into performance marketing execution across channels such as Search, Paid Social, and Affiliates.
Build dashboards, tools, and documentation to improve operational efficiency and enable self-serve reporting across teams.
Identify automation and process improvements to reduce manual work and scale operational workflows.
Support cross-functional business reviews with clear insights and structured analysis.


Requirements


2-5 years of experience in e-commerce, category management, marketing operations, performance marketing, or commercial analytics.
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to structure complex data into clear, actionable insights.
Experience working with multiple stakeholders and managing parallel workstreams in fast-paced environments.
Highly organized, detail-oriented, and operationally strong with excellent execution discipline.
Strong communication skills and ability to collaborate across marketing, supply, and commercial teams.
Proactive, problem-solving mindset with a focus on ownership and delivery.
Familiarity with performance marketing channels such as Search, Shopping, Paid Social, or Affiliates is a plus.
Experience in marketplace environments or consumer electronics is an advantage.


Benefits


Competitive salary aligned with experience.
Fully remote-first working model with strong flexibility.
Workation opportunities of up to two months per year.
Learning and development budget for courses, conferences, and professional growth.
Access to mental health and personal development support programs.
Collaborative, feedback-driven culture focused on autonomy and impact.
Regular internal workshops and cross-team learning initiatives.
Office weeks and optional in-person team gatherings several times per year.


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. description_full:
Hi, we’re AMBOSS and we’re searching for a Performance Marketing Manager to support our growth in International Markets!




About AMBOSS

AMBOSS is the copilot for medical professionals, empowering them to provide the best possible care through our learning and clinical decision support tool. Our team brings together care, innovative thinking, and cutting-edge AI medical technology. Our founders launched AMBOSS in 2012 with the goal of creating a tool they wished they had during medical school and clinical practice. Since then, we've grown to over 600 employees and are active in over 180 countries - primarily in Germany and the US. Our offices are located in Berlin, Cologne, New York, and Cagliari.




Want to learn more about our culture? Watch our video to explore what makes us unique.




Why can this position be exciting for you?

As a Performance Marketing Manager at AMBOSS, you’ll shape how future doctors and students around the world discover our product. You’ll have a direct impact on our acquisition across different countries and deliver growth while ensuring profitability.

You’ll collaborate with a cross-functional team that values creativity, experimentation, and results. With strong tools and the freedom to test and iterate, your work will directly influence how users engage and convert to AMBOSS customers.

If you’re passionate about creating data-informed digital campaigns from A to Z —and want your work to have a real-world impact—this is the role for you.




You will:

Own and scale paid media campaigns across platforms like Google Ads, Meta, TikTok, and Apple Search Ads—ensuring campaigns are aligned with business objectives and tailored to local markets (International Student, IMG (International Medical Graduates, International Clinicians)
Manage budgets, forecasts, and pacing to maximize return on ad spend (ROAS) while hitting volume targets
Develop and run A/B tests on creatives, landing pages, and audience strategies to drive conversion improvements
Analyze campaign performance using platforms like GA4, Looker, and channel dashboards, and turn insights into actionable recommendations
Collaborate cross-functionally with product marketing, creative, web, and local teams to ensure message and funnel consistency
Report on KPIs (e.g., CPA, LTV, ROAS, CR) and drive regular performance reviews with stakeholders




You bring:

Channel expertise: Hands-on experience managing paid campaigns across Google Ads, Meta, TikTok, and Apple Search Ads
Data-driven mindset: Confident working with performance metrics (ROAS, CPA, LTV, CTR, CR) and tools like GA4, Looker, Excel/Sheets, and attribution platforms
Testing & optimization skills: Strong knowledge of A/B testing, funnel analysis, and conversion rate optimization (CRO) best practices
Strategic thinking: Ability to connect campaign performance to broader business goals and adjust strategy accordingly
Collaboration & communication: Comfortable working cross-functionally with marketing, creative, web, and analytics teams to align efforts and drive results
Creative judgment: You know how to brief, evaluate, and iterate on ad creatives to improve performance
Growth mindset: Proactive, curious, and eager to learn—always looking for ways to improve processes and outcomes




You enjoy:

Turning data and insights into decisions—and seeing the direct impact of your campaigns on business growth
Solving problems through experimentation, iteration, and optimization
Collaborating with creative, product, and marketing teams to build user-centric campaigns
Working in a fast-paced environment where curiosity and initiative are valued
Staying up to date on performance trends, platform updates, and growth strategies
Taking ownership of your channels and driving them forward with autonomy and purpose

description_full:
BESTSECRET is the leading European online destination for premium and luxury off-price fashion. The Group offers its members in 27 countries across Europe a superior brand portfolio complemented by a high-end shopping experience. At the same time, BESTSECRET enables its brand partners to clear overstock at scale with minimum visibility and maximum brand equity protection. The unique business model is based on its closed character with an invitation-only customer membership, longstanding brand partner and customer relationships, combined with a tech-focused mindset. BESTSECRET has a strong track record of profitable growth over many years. It generated preliminary revenues of approx. EUR 1,415 million in 2024 and employs around 2,300 people from over 90 nations. The Group draws on a heritage of 100 years in the textile and fashion industry and today is owned by Permira Funds and the founding families Schustermann and Borenstein.

Your Responsibilities


Drive and coordinate weekly short‑ and mid‑term pricing activities for Retail Stores, closely aligned with current market, competitive and stock situations
Define and continuously refine country‑, category‑ and store‑specific pricing strategies, considering local market dynamics and retail specifics
Ensure an efficient, scalable and secure end‑to‑end pricing operation, covering tens of thousands of price changes per week across store networks
Monitor and analyze competitor pricing in offline retail, and translate insights into clear pricing actions that balance competitiveness and profitability
Analyze and interpret price elasticity and demand response in a retail context, proactively leveraging insights to steer pricing decisions
Partner with Buying, Retail Operations, Supply and Analytics teams to ensure pricing decisions are well understood and executed in store


Your Qualifications


Minimum 3 years of professional experience in pricing, revenue management or a comparable analytical role within B2C Fashion Retail or E‑Commerce; hands‑on Retail experience is a strong advantage
Strong analytical and quantitative skillset, including price analysis, forecasting, scenario simulation and optimization
Proven ability to work with large, complex datasets and translate data into actionable business recommendations (e.g. Power BI, SQL)
Solid understanding of commercial KPIs and the trade‑offs between margin, sell‑through, competitiveness and inventory risk
High level of ownership and proactivity – able to work independently, prioritize effectively and drive topics to completion in a complex organization
Strong stakeholder management & communication skills, with the ability to explain analytical insights clearly to diverse audiences, including senior management
Fluent business English (spoken and written); German language skills are a plus, especially in a retail environment


At BESTSECRET, diversity isn't just a trend – it's our professional philosophy. We are dedicated to cultivating an inclusive workplace where everyone's unique talents and backgrounds are celebrated. Applicants from all backgrounds, experiences and perspectives are welcome to join us in our mission towards a more vibrant and inclusive future.

description_full:
About AMBOSS

AMBOSS is the copilot for medical professionals, empowering them to provide the best possible care through our learning and clinical decision support tool. Our team brings together care, innovative thinking, and cutting-edge AI medical technology. Our founders launched AMBOSS in 2012 with the goal of creating a tool they wished they had during medical school and clinical practice. Since then, we've grown to over 600 employees and are active in over 180 countries - primarily in Germany and the US. Our offices are located in Berlin, Cologne, New York, Cagliari and Cape Town.




Want to learn more about our culture? Watch our video to explore what makes us unique.




Why can this position be exciting for you?




The FP&A Analyst at AMBOSS plays a crucial role in guiding our financial planning and analysis activities, driving strategic decision-making, and providing insights that enhance our company's financial performance. Drawing upon your experience in financial management, you will collaborate closely with cross-functional teams to ensure financial goals are met and long-term business strategies are effectively supported. The primary objectives of this role are to drive financial excel.




You will:

Support the annual budgeting process, regular forecasting, and long-term financial planning
Analyze financial performance against targets, highlighting key trends, risks, and opportunities, and translating findings into clear, actionable insights
Build financial models and scenario analyses to evaluate business initiatives and support planning decisions
Partner with business units and department leads to understand performance drivers and help align financial plans with operational goals
Provide financial insights to support decision-making across the organization, acting as a reliable point of contact for finance-related topics
Strengthen collaboration with stakeholders to improve financial transparency, information flow, and planning processes




You bring:

A strong academic background in Business Administration, Economics, Engineering, Business Informatics, or a related field.
1-2 years of experience in FP&A, business analytics, consulting, or a similar role in a fast-paced environment
Strong interest in understanding SaaS business models, unit economics, and common growth metrics
Advanced spreadsheet skills (especially Google Sheets), including complex formulas, and confidence working with data; experience with BI tools, SQL, or planning/reporting systems is a plus
An analytical mindset with the ability to turn complex data into clear insights that support decision-making
Clear and structured communication skills, with the ability to explain financial topics to non-finance stakeholders
A well-organized and reliable self-starter, able to proactively take ownership of tasks and manage priorities in a dynamic environment.
A collaborative approach and a pragmatic, hands-on attitude
Curiosity about the product, customers, and market, and a motivation to continuously learn and grow




You enjoy:

Turning numbers into meaningful insights - and seeing how your analysis shapes strategic decisions across the company
Building financial models and scenarios that bring clarity to complex business questions
Partnering with stakeholders across teams to understand what drives performance and translating that into robust financial plans
Creating structure in ambiguity and improving planning, forecasting, and reporting processes
Diving deep into SaaS metrics, unit economics, and growth drivers to uncover risks and opportunities
Balancing big-picture thinking with hands-on execution - switching seamlessly between strategy discussions and spreadsheet work
Taking ownership of your work and being a trusted sparring partner for finance and non-finance colleagues alike
Contributing to a mission-driven organization where your work directly supports better medical education and ultimately better patient care




Benefits:




AMBOSSians tell us that innovative work keeps them energized and employee benefits help them to feel appreciated and empowered. We invest in every AMBOSSian with our employee benefits package, crafted to support financial, physical, and mental health, and work-life harmony.

AML Analyst (m/f/d)



📍 Stuttgart | Full-time | Hybrid



About the Role



For our retail banking AML function, we are looking for a AML Officer to support day-to-day activities across customer onboarding, transaction monitoring, and financial crime prevention. This is an excellent opportunity for candidates with a banking or finance background who want to further develop their expertise in AML, KYC, and fraud prevention within a regulated banking environment.



Key Responsibilities


Support customer onboarding and ongoing due diligence (KYC/CDD/EDD) processes
Review and assess customer documentation in line with AML and regulatory standards
Conduct transaction monitoring, including identification and investigation of unusual or suspicious activity
Perform sanctions and watchlist screening, including escalations where required
Support anti-fraud checks and AML investigations
Document findings clearly and maintain accurate records in internal systems
Collaborate with internal stakeholders such as Compliance, Operations, and Front Office
Support regulatory and internal audit requests where necessary


Your Profile


Completed banking apprenticeship (Bankkaufmann/-frau) and/or a degree in Business Administration, Finance, Economics, or a related field
Initial professional experience in customer onboarding, KYC, AML, or compliance within a bank or financial institution, ideally in retail banking
Basic understanding of German and EU regulatory requirements relating to AML, KYC, sanctions, and fraud (e.g. GwG, BaFin requirements)
Familiarity with topics such as transaction monitoring, sanctions screening, fraud checks, and AML investigations
Strong attention to detail with a structured and reliable working style
Ability to analyse information and make well-reasoned risk-based assessments
Good communication skills and confidence working in a regulated environment
Fluent in German; good English skills are an advantage


Interested?



If you are looking to build or deepen your career in AML and financial crime prevention within retail banking, we look forward to receiving your application.

Freelance | 8–20 hrs/week | Remote (EU/UK)

Are you an experienced market analyst with a sharp eye for industry trends and an understanding of data-driven decision-making? Do you have 8 to 20 hours per week available alongside your main job or consulting projects? Join us to shape how AI comprehends and applies market analysis expertise at scale.

10x.team connects expert freelancers with leading AI labs building next-generation models. We are seeking knowledgeable market analysts based in the EU or UK to enhance the accuracy, depth, and real-world relevance of AI-powered business and market research systems.

Review and refine AI-generated outputs related to market analysis, business insights, data interpretation, and the practical aspects of market research workflows

Skilled at evaluating market strategies, documentation, and quality standards
Comfortable working independently and providing structured, critical feedback

Flexible, fully remote freelance work that fits your current commitments

description_full:
About Agoda

At Agoda, we bridge the world through travel. Our story began in 2005, when two lifelong friends and entrepreneurs, driven by their passion for travel, launched Agoda to make it easier for everyone to explore the world.

Today, we are part of Booking Holdings [NASDAQ: BKNG], with a diverse team of over 7,000 people from 90 countries, working together in offices around the globe. Every day, we connect people to destinations and experiences, with our great deals across our millions of hotels and holiday properties, flights, and experiences worldwide.

No two days are the same at Agoda. Data and technology are at the heart of our culture, fueling our curiosity and innovation. If you’re ready to begin your best journey and help build travel for the world, join us.

Get to Know our Team:

Agoda's Brand Marketing team is looking for an Analyst to scale our digital marketing & brand research efforts.

As a part of the Brand Media & Measurement team, you will manage digital campaign planning, A/B testing, and reporting across key digital channels like Meta, Twitter, YouTube etc., and also be responsible for management & analysis of brand research projects.

This person will have the opportunity to learn and grow with the global Brand Marketing team based in Thailand. They will play a key role in analyzing all kind of campaign & research data to deliver insights that will shape the team’s strategy and fulfil our ambition of creating an engaging and meaningful brand in the tech-and-travel space.

This role is based out of Agoda’s Global Operations Center in Bangkok, Thailand, giving the successful applicant the once-in-a-lifetime chance to work in one of the world’s most exciting and fast-paced cities, close to some of the most iconic travel destinations, with a fun and talented team. We provide complete relocation for you and your family and excellent benefits.

About The Brand Team

We are the guardians of the Agoda brand, with a mission to make Agoda a trusted name in global travel. Our scope is broad and encompasses various projects and collaborations with other departments at Agoda. In any given week, we could be working on campaign creation and activation (including consumer research, strategy, agency management, media planning, measurement, and testing), producing assets for Agoda’s various marketing channels and our website and app, or designing official Agoda merch for our offices around the globe. We ensure the brand is always looking and sounding its best in everything we do, whether on the world stage or in a company newsletter.

The Brand team is diverse, representing strategists, media planners, project managers, brand managers, creatives, and more, from different walks of life. We have in common a love of travel (and travel deals) and a passion for finding fun and innovative ways to bring the Agoda brand to the world.

In this role, you’ll get to:


Experiment: Conduct A/B testing on platforms like Facebook, Instagram, Twitter, TikTok and YouTube, as well as brand research in-house & with external agencies to demonstrate the impact of campaign activity on brand awareness.
Execute: Own the performance of digital & social campaigns, optimize critical campaign metrics, and keep an eye on data to ensure that media is delivering against the plan.
Learn: Communicate test results & campaign learnings to the team and broader stakeholders through comprehensive analysis, reporting and data visualization. Develop key learnings for different media platforms and markets.
Analyse: Delve into data from digital tests & research studies to gather useful insights for optimization of brand campaigns.
Collaborate: Liaise with external media partners (e.g. Google, Meta) for planning, analysis and execution, and consolidate media data and analysis with digital activity being run in-house.


What you’ll need to succeed:


A minimum of a bachelor’s degree in mathematics, marketing, communication, business, or another related field
At least 2 years of experience in analytically focused positions
Great SQL & Tableau knowledge
Advanced knowledge of Excel, very comfortable using VLOOKUP and Pivot Tables
Great project management, measurement, and planning capability; taking ownership from the start to the finish
Knowledge of statistics and data processing practices (calculating p-values, data cleaning and normalization, regressions, etc.) is a plus

description_full:
About HOLY


HOLY is the soft drink revolution: With our powdered drinks HOLY Energy®, HOLY Hydration®, HOLY Iced Tea®, and HOLY Milkshake® you get amazing taste across 50+ flavors while maintaining a good conscience—for your body & the environment.
Over 2M loyal customers: Started in a shared apartment by three best friends, we have scaled to Europe's fastest growing consumer brand—and our cult-like community, the HOLY Squad, loves it: 4.6 stars on reviews.io speak for themselves.
From D2C to Global Omnichannel: In less than four years, we've profitably scaled HOLY to more than €150M D2C revenue in DACH, FR, UK and PL. Now scaling our retail business, we're on an exciting transition to becoming a true omnichannel rocketship.
We are still just getting started: Backed by the world's best consumer investors, we aim to revolutionize the soft drink industry and build a generational love brand: tastier, healthier & more sustainable. Our team of 150+ people aims high (Coca-Cola, we're coming for you!).


About The Position

As a Marketing Analyst at HOLY, you'll be the analytical backbone of our marketing and growth teams. Your mission: Turn data into decisions by delivering accurate forecasts, actionable insights, and performance analysis that fuel HOLY's commercial engine.

Responsibilities:

Sales Forecasting & Budget Tracking


Own weekly revenue forecasts and maintain our driver-based forecasting model to keep expectations aligned with reality.
Track marketing spend and budget pacing, ensuring optimal allocation across channels and campaigns.
Partner with Finance and Growth teams to optimize HOLY's commercial planning and resource allocation.


Ad-hoc Analytics & Campaign Performance Insights


Deliver rapid-response analysis for marketing teams, including campaign debriefs, performance deep dives, and channel troubleshooting.
Build and maintain dashboards that empower teams to make faster, smarter decisions
Turn complex data into clear, actionable recommendations that drive ROI and customer acquisition efficiency.


Incrementality & Marketing Channel Analytics


Design and execute incrementality tests to measure true marketing effectiveness across channels.
Provide data-driven recommendations to improve marketing ROI and customer acquisition strategy.
Analyze channel performance, identify optimization opportunities, and support strategic decision-making.


Order Volume Forecasting


Build and maintain short-term demand forecasts that factor in promotional impact and offering changes.
Model campaign volume scenarios to support inventory planning and fulfillment operations.
Help optimize resource allocation across the business by connecting marketing activity to operational needs.


Cross-Functional Operating Cadence


Prepare weekly and monthly performance meetings with clear, compelling analysis that supports management decisions.
Collaborate closely with Marketing, CRM, Finance, E-Commerce, and Operations teams.
Proactively flag risks, spot opportunities, and drive follow-through on your recommendations.


About You


Customer and business intuition: Connecting data to HOLY's product, customer behavior, and business model comes naturally, supported by 2–4 years of hands-on experience in analytics, BI, or data science (ideally in e-commerce, D2C, or consumer goods).
Analytical rigor meets speed: A strong 80/20 mindset helps you balance depth and pace, delivering fast and reliable analysis without unnecessary complexity. You have a proven ability to build forecasting models, run incrementality testing, and turn performance data into actionable insights.
Technical proficiency: Proficiency in SQL and advanced spreadsheet modeling (Google Sheets or Excel) is expected, along with experience in BI tools like Metabase, Looker, or Tableau. Bonus points for Python or R.
Clear communicator: Complex insights are translated into crisp, compelling stories for different audiences and stakeholders. Fluency in English is required.
Ownership mindset: A proactive approach to spotting issues, flagging them early, and driving recommendations through to action is essential. You do not wait to be asked twice.


Our Offering

In today's job market, A-players have many options to choose from. As part of our offer, we prioritise providing a productive working environment and opportunities for growth. In addition to competitive compensation, including company shares, you can expect:


A successful and growing company: Join a company that has been growing profitable and quickly for the last 36+ months and have raised a series B, while many other e-commerce companies are struggling.
Huge impact: Enjoy huge impact to shape HOLY’s internationalisation in a top market.
Awesome team: Join an awesome team of colleagues who are ambitious, funny, down-to-earth, and authentic. Get to know us and see for yourself!
Top product: Marketing is important, but a good product is essential. Our differentiated soft drinks have won over 2.5 million customers. Check out our reviews.io reviews or contact us for free samples.
Startup culture: Join a startup culture that values flat hierarchies, fast pace, and personal development. With us, you can make a real impact.
Perfect setup: Enjoy a perfect setup that includes all the requirements of a modern employer, such as a central office in Berlin Prenzlauerberg, flexible working hours, and much more.
Competitive comp. + equity: Join us at a pivotal stage of hypergrowth. We offer a strong salary and meaningful equity—giving you the chance to share in our current success and benefit from the massive upside as we scale.
How we support your growth: flexible hours, 28 vacation days, 30 work-from-abroad days, €1,500 learning budget, Urban Sports Club membership, public transport subsidy, and biannual 360° feedback tied to your compensation.


Find out more about HOLY and our team on our careers page.

The Process

As we are experiencing rapid growth and have ambitious plans, the optimal start date for the position is today. Nonetheless, we are prepared to patiently await the arrival of the ideal candidate, recognizing that quality ultimately yields the best results.


Getting to know you: You will have a 30 minute screening interview with Eric (Talent Acquisition Manager), where we will talk to you more about HOLY and the position and see if our expectations match.
Hiring Manager interview with Martijn: Diving deeper into some more technical topics and your experience with Martijn our Sr. Business Operations & Analytics, our Sr. Talent Development Manager.
Case study & on-site interview: As soon as you have successfully completed the first interviews, you will receive a case study. In a second meeting at our office, the team will discuss your case study with you and answer questions about our workspace, the HOLY Temple.
Founders Interview: As a strong cultural fit is important to us, you will have a final meeting with Phil , one of our HOLY Founders. This gives both you and us the opportunity for a final check.
Job offer and decision: If everything goes well, you will receive an offer and we are ready to embark you this journey together with you!


Typically his process should take around 2 weeks.

Any questions or concerns?

If you have any questions or feedback, please send an email to jobs@holy.com.

description_full:
Based In Frankfurt

2 days working from home, three days in the office




The Change Partners are working with a global leader in the home wellness and sleep technology sector. We are looking for an ambitious, "pricing-first" Global Performance & Value Manager to join their Category Performance, Pricing & Market Intelligence team in Lisbon.




You will be the bridge between complex data and actionable strategy. Your mission is to provide the data, frameworks, and strategic insights that make better portfolio decisions possible across the full product lifecycle.




Your Responsibilities:




Category Performance Intelligence: Design and own global performance dashboards in partnership with BI teams, tracking revenue, margin, and NPS correlation by SKU and channel.




Pricing Architecture: Design and maintain global price ladders, pricing tiers, and trade-up logic to ensure portfolio coherence across all market clusters.
Value Capture: Maintain a pricing elasticity model and conduct regular price-value assessments to ensure products are priced relative to consumer-perceived value.




Promotional Governance: Define promo guardrails and build tracking models to measure actual ROI vs. planned, protecting the brand from margin erosion.
Competitive Intelligence: Own a structured view of the competitive landscape, tracking rival pricing, launches, and promotional activity across key markets.




Market Intelligence: Develop repeatable processes for market sizing and category growth analysis to identify white-space opportunities and strategic risks.




Business Case Support: Serve as the analytical partner to Global and Regional Category Managers, building validated business cases for new product investments and expansion.




Analytics Empowerment: Develop self-service tools and templates that help the broader category team make faster, data-backed decisions.




What We’re Looking For:




Experience: 5–8 years in pricing, business analytics, revenue management, or category performance within a consumer goods, DTC, or e-commerce context.




Pricing Strategy: A proven track record of designing pricing frameworks and understanding the interplay between pricing, perceived value, and margin.




Data Literacy: Highly analytical mindset with the ability to translate complex data into clear, decision-ready business recommendations.




Statistical Foundation: Solid understanding of statistics (hypothesis testing, regressions) and clustering methods like k-means.




Data Visualisation: Proven experience building performance dashboards in BI tools such as Tableau, Power BI, or Looker.




Technical Proficiency: Working knowledge of Python for data analysis (pandas, data cleaning, and exploratory analysis).




Please note this role is based in Frankfurt, and you’ll need the full right to work in Germany

Relocation package support is not available

Standorte: Berlin, Düsseldorf, Frankfurt (Main), Hamburg, Hannover, Köln, München und Stuttgart.

Dein Impact:

Als Manager Data Architecture & Analytics (m/w/d) führst du Projektteams bei der End-to-End Implementierung komplexer Enterprise-Datenlösungen zum Erfolg und sorgst für echten Mehrwert bei unseren Kunden.


Projektleitung & Delivery: Du verantwortest die erfolgreiche Umsetzung von AI & Data Projekten unter Einhaltung von Zeitplänen, Budgets und Qualitätsstandards.

description_full:
This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for a Product Manager, Growth & Analytics, Beeper in Germany.

This role sits at the core of product growth strategy, where data, experimentation, and execution converge to shape user behavior and business outcomes. You will take ownership of growth initiatives, identifying what drives activation, retention, and monetization while continuously refining the product experience. Working closely with leadership and engineering teams, you will turn ambiguous product questions into measurable experiments and actionable insights. The environment is highly analytical and fast-moving, requiring strong independence and intellectual rigor. You will directly influence how users engage with the product and how value is captured across the user journey. This is a high-ownership role for a product thinker who is equally comfortable in data, strategy, and execution.

Accountabilities


Own end-to-end product execution for growth initiatives, including defining specs, user stories, success criteria, and experiment frameworks
Design, run, and analyze experiments from hypothesis to launch to results interpretation, ensuring clear learning outcomes
Build and maintain core product dashboards covering activation, retention, conversion, and engagement metrics used by leadership
Conduct deep-dive analysis when key metrics shift, identifying root causes and recommending actionable solutions
Collaborate with engineering and support teams to validate product behavior and confirm the impact of shipped features
Maintain accurate and up-to-date product documentation, including specifications, release notes, and internal references
Translate data insights into product decisions and communicate findings clearly across technical and non-technical stakeholders


Requirements


4-6 years of experience in product management, growth, analytics, or a closely related role with full ownership of product execution
Strong SQL skills including CTEs, window functions, cohort analysis, and funnel-based querying (required)
Experience working with cloud data warehouses and building dashboards or recurring analytical reports
Ability to write clear product specifications that guide engineering execution and data-driven summaries for leadership
Hands-on experience running experiments and understanding the full lifecycle of product testing and learning
Strong analytical mindset with the ability to interpret complex datasets and translate them into product decisions
Familiarity with A/B testing frameworks and experiment design methodologies
Experience with consumer, messaging, or subscription-based products is a plus
Ability to work with imperfect or incomplete data while maintaining analytical rigor
Strong communication skills and ability to operate independently in a fast-paced, distributed team environment


Benefits


Competitive salary range of $120,000-$190,000 USD (adjusted for location and paid in local currency)
Fully remote work with global flexibility and autonomy
Open vacation policy supporting work-life balance
Opportunity to work on high-impact consumer products at global scale
Strong ownership culture with direct visibility into leadership decisions
Collaborative, distributed team environment across multiple time zones
Exposure to modern growth, analytics, and experimentation frameworks
Inclusive, mission-driven organization focused on open web and digital communication


How Jobgether Works

We use an AI-powered matching process to ensure your application is reviewed quickly, objectively, and fairly against the role's core requirements. Our system identifies the top-fitting candidates, and this shortlist is then shared directly with the hiring company. The final decision and next steps (interviews, assessments) are managed by their internal team.

We appreciate your interest and wish you the best!

Why Apply Through Jobgether?

Data Privacy Notice: By submitting your application, you acknowledge that Jobgether will process your personal data to evaluate your candidacy and share relevant information with the hiring employer. This processing is based on legitimate interest and pre-contractual measures under applicable data protection laws (including GDPR). You may exercise your rights (access, rectification, erasure, objection) at any time.

We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. description_full:
Your Mission




We’re looking for a Performance Marketing Manager who wants to do more than just run campaigns — someone who’s excited to directly shape how companies source and manufacture parts through MakerVerse. In this role, you’ll own our digital acquisition engine end-to-end, turning high-intent B2B demand into real business impact. From the first search to the final order, you’ll optimize every step of the funnel across Search, PMax, and Display — driving qualified leads and revenue for our Account Executives.




Your Impact




Drive Growth: Execute and optimize paid media campaigns to accelerate customer acquisition and order entry on makerverse.com.
Funnel Optimization: Collaborate with the Platform team to ensure web events are correctly tracked in Mixpanel and HubSpot, enabling a seamless data flow.
Sales Enablement: Work closely with the Sales team to ensure campaigns target the right industries (e.g. Aerospace, MedTech) and attract high-value decision-makers.
Conversion Optimization: Build and optimize landing pages in Unbounce and WordPress to maximize conversion rates from paid traffic.
Paid Search Focus: Continuously improve campaign structure, targeting, and performance across Search, PMax, and Display to capture high-intent demand.
Collaboration: Maintain a strong feedback loop across teams, sharing insights to continuously improve performance.







Your DNA




Solid hands-on experience managing Google Ads campaigns (Search, PMax, Display), including setup, optimization, and performance ownership. You have worked independently on campaigns and are confident managing them end-to-end.
You are comfortable working with data and deriving actionable insights. Experience with GA4 and/or Mixpanel is a strong plus.
Familiarity with tools like GTM, HubSpot, or Unbounce to support tracking, campaign setup, and landing page optimization.
You understand how marketing contributes to pipeline and enjoy collaborating with Sales to improve lead quality and conversion.
Hands-on experience with A/B testing and a strong drive to continuously improve CPA, conversion rates, and overall performance.
Professional English required. German is a strong plus given our core market.







Join Us & Raise the Bar




At MakerVerse, we don’t just execute — we innovate. We don’t just meet expectations — we exceed them. If you’re ready to take ownership, tackle meaningful challenges, and grow as part of a strong team, we’d love to hear from you.




Our team lives by these principles:




We Own It. We Deliver It. – We take responsibility, act with purpose, and go the extra mile to create impact.
We Execute Relentlessly. We Solve Big Challenges. – We embrace a “Doer” mindset & focus on solutions, not problems.
Our Customers’ Success = Our Success. – We solve for impact & prioritize what drives customer success.
We Win Together. We Set the Bar High. – We challenge and support each other to achieve our best and succeed as a high-performance team.

Wir sind hochprofitabel, verfügen über herausragende Produkte und verkaufen bereits weltweit über unsere bestehenden Shops – und das ist erst der Anfang. Dafür suchen wir einen Performance-Marketing-Profi mit echtem E-Commerce-Verständnis: Du verwaltest nicht nur Budgets, sondern denkst Performance ganzheitlich, von der Ad bis zur Conversion im Shop.

description_full:
Performance Marketing Manager – Amazon PPC (m/w/d)

Du denkst in Klicks, Conversions und ROAS – und weißt genau, welchen Hebel du wann ansetzen musst? Dann bist du bei uns genau richtig. Als Performance Marketing Manager mit Schwerpunkt Amazon PPC übernimmst du die Verantwortung für bezahlte Kampagnen unserer Kunden auf Amazon und weiteren Marktplätzen – datengetrieben, strategisch und mit echtem Impact.



Über OMNIFOX

OMNIFOX ist keine klassische Agentur. Wir wurden von ehemaligen Amazon-Mitarbeitern gegründet, die zusammen über 12 Jahre Erfahrung bei Amazon gesammelt haben – von Vendor Management über Advertising bis zur strategischen Marktentwicklung. Heute sind wir ein 15-köpfiges Team aus E-Commerce-Experten, Performance-Marketern und Amazon-Spezialisten, mit Sitz im Herzen Münchens und flexibler Remote-Arbeit.

Was uns antreibt: echtes Insider-Wissen statt Marketing-Versprechen. Wir kennen Algorithmen, Entscheidungswege und Best Practices von innen – und setzen dieses Wissen gezielt für unsere Kunden ein.

Unser Portfolio reicht von Amazon PPC & DSP über Vendor und Seller Management bis hin zu Retail Media: Wir schalten, neben den offenen Marktplätzen, Sponsored Ads (SPADs) auch auf geschlossenen Marktplätzen für die größte Drogeriekette Europas und eine der führenden Sportartikelketten – und gehören damit zu den erfahrensten Retail Media Agenturen im deutschsprachigen Raum. Zuletzt waren wir als Top-3-Finalist bei den Amazon Ads Partner Awards 2025 nominiert.

Wir wachsen weiter – und suchen Menschen, die mit uns wachsen wollen.



Deine Aufgaben

Kampagnenmanagement: Du planst, steuerst und optimierst Amazon Ads Kampagnen (Sponsored Products, Sponsored Brands, Sponsored Display) eigenständig für einen festen Kundenstamm.

Performance-Strategie: Du entwickelst individuelle PPC-Strategien zur Umsatzsteigerung und Effizienzoptimierung – mit klarem Blick auf ACoS, TACoS und ROAS.

Keyword- & Gebotsmanagement: Du führst fundierte Keyword-Recherchen durch, strukturierst Kampagnen sauber und steuerst Gebote auf Basis realer Performance-Daten.

Analyse & Optimierung: Du wertest Kampagnendaten systematisch aus, erkennst Potenziale frühzeitig und leitest konkrete Maßnahmen ab.

Reporting & Kommunikation: Du erstellst aussagekräftige Performance-Reports und präsentierst Ergebnisse sowie Handlungsempfehlungen direkt bei unseren Kunden.

Kanalübergreifendes Denken: Du verzahnst PPC-Maßnahmen mit Content- und Listing-Optimierungen für maximale Sichtbarkeit und Conversion.




Dein Profil

Du hast nachweisbare Erfahrung im Amazon PPC Management – idealerweise auf Agentur- oder Seller/Vendor-Seite.
Du kennst den Amazon Ads Manager in- und auswendig und arbeitest sicher mit AI-Tools.
Du bist zahlenaffin, analysierst gerne und verlierst dabei nie das strategische Ziel aus dem Blick.
Du kommunizierst sicher auf Deutsch und Englisch – intern wie im Kundenkontakt.
Du arbeitest strukturiert, eigenverantwortlich und behältst auch bei mehreren Accounts den Überblick.
Kenntnisse in Retail Media oder weiteren Paid-Kanälen sind ein Plus, aber kein Muss.




Was dich bei uns erwartet

Remote-First: 100 % Homeoffice oder unser Office in München – du entscheidest.
Weiterentwicklung: Zugang zu Schulungen, Zertifizierungen (u. a. Amazon Ads) und echten Karriereperspektiven.
Teamkultur: Flache Hierarchien, kurze Entscheidungswege und ein Team, das Performance wirklich lebt.
Top-Ausstattung: Moderne Tools und Technologien, die deine Arbeit effizienter machen.
Benefits: Flexible Arbeitszeiten, Firmenevents und ein Mentoring-Programm.




Rahmenbedingungen

Art der Stelle: Vollzeit, Festanstellung
Arbeitszeiten: Montag bis Freitag
Sprache: Deutsch erforderlich
Arbeitsort: 100 % Remote oder München
